cards.title: Dutch Walking Festival Boosts Child Happiness and Health
cards.body: The Avondvierdaagse is an annual four-night walking festival where Dutch children, parents, and teachers explore neighbourhoods together. The tradition promotes exercise, friendship-building, and appears genuinely transformative for child wellbeing.

cards.title: Baby Pensions: New Parental Savings Pressure
cards.body: Parents are increasingly being encouraged to open self-invested personal pensions for their children. This adds another financial guilt to already stretched households where millions struggle with their own retirement savings.

cards.title: AI Co-Parenting: The New Parental Support Tool
cards.body: Some parents are using AI to help manage caregiving tasks as women in heterosexual marriages continue shouldering most childcare responsibilities. The trend highlights ongoing imbalances in household parenting roles.

cards.title: Helping Children Transition to Full-Time School
cards.body: Six-year-olds need strong social skills to succeed in full-time school peer interactions. However, their unique backgrounds may create obstacles that require thoughtful support.

cards.title: Do Controlling Parents Create Aggressive Children?
cards.body: Research examines whether overly controlling parenting styles can lead to aggressive behaviour in children. Understanding this dynamic helps parents find balanced discipline approaches.

cards.title: The Rise of Beta Parenting: Relaxed Over Intensive
cards.body: The 'beta mom' trend rejects intensive, highly managed parenting for more relaxed approaches. This shift represents a return to more natural, less anxious parenting styles.

cards.title: Stepmother-Stepdaughter Relationships: Navigating Tension
cards.body: Many stepmother-stepdaughter relationships struggle with distance, insecurity, and jealousy. Understanding these dynamics helps families build healthier blended-family bonds.

cards.title: Autistic Children Need Protection From Unproven Treatments
cards.body: Relaxing standards for autistic children can expose them to untested interventions. Safe, evidence-based approaches should always guide childhood care decisions.

cards.title: UK Announces Social Media Ban for Under-16s
cards.body: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a ban preventing under-16s from accessing major social media platforms. This policy aims to protect children's mental health and wellbeing.

cards.title: Hungary's Birth Rate Experiment: Limited Success
cards.body: Hungary's pronatalist policies initially boosted birth rates but the gains didn't sustain long-term. The outcomes offer lessons for other countries attempting to increase fertility.
